title: Measure the Environmental Impact of AI

slides.title: Measure the Environmental Impact of AI
slides.subtitle: A proactive approach to effective monitoring and initiatives
slides.speaker_note: AI value and AI footprint are now inseparable.
slides.visual: Split-screen data center and sustainability dashboard

slides.title: Why this matters now
bullets: AI workload growth raises electricity demand
Regulatory and customer pressure for transparent disclosure
Cost and emissions optimization can align
slides.visual: Trend chart: AI compute growth vs sustainability targets

slides.title: AI supply-chain footprint
bullets: Data storage: continuous power, replication, tiering
Data centres: PUE, local air pollution, water usage (WUE) and water stress
Model training: bursty energy + embodied hardware emissions
Inference at scale: small per-request cost x billions of requests
slides.visual: Supply-chain map with energy/carbon/water/air badges per stage

slides.title: Impact dimensions to track
bullets: Air quality (local pollution from generation and grid mix)

slides.title: Core KPIs
bullets: Ops: telemetry and alert response coverage

slides.title: Measurement architecture
bullets: Join with infra power, PUE, and WUE

slides.title: Instrumentation choices
bullets: Data pipelines: runtime and I/O metrics

slides.title: Tackling energy use: high-impact initiatives
bullets: Retire low-value models and storage pipelines

slides.title: Scaling AI while balancing sustainability
bullets: Efficiency as a first-class KPI: decouple growth from footprint
Carbon- and water-aware placement for new capacity
Tiered model strategy: small models for easy requests
Shared/reusable models and caching over redundant retraining
slides.visual: Decoupling curve: rising volume vs falling per-request intensity

slides.title: Risks and mitigations
bullets: Tool sprawl -> single source of truth

references: International Energy Agency (IEA). Electricity analysis and AI/data center updates. https://www.iea.org/
Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC). AR6 Synthesis Report. https://www.ipcc.ch/report/ar6/syr/
GHG Protocol. Corporate Standard and Scope 2 Guidance. https://ghgprotocol.org/
International Organization for Standardization (ISO). ISO 14064-1 overview. https://www.iso.org/standard/66453.html
image_prompts: Modern AI data center with sustainability dashboard overlay, clean corporate style
Minimal vector diagram of AI telemetry to carbon accounting pipeline
Executive dashboard showing emissions intensity trends and anomaly alerts
guiding_questions: What is the environmental impact across the AI supply chain (data storage, data centres, model training), including energy, carbon, air pollution, and water usage?
How can organisations tackle energy-usage concerns and bring sustainability to the forefront of responsible-AI discussions?
How do organisations encourage AI scalability while balancing sustainability concerns?
